summary.intent: Implement Gemini thought_signature round-tripping via a metadata field on ToolCall (#375) and strengthen schema normalization to handle typeless required properties for Google AI Studio (#793).
summary.outcome: Both features were fully implemented with unit tests, all 4964 tests passing, clippy clean, and a PR (#795) created and merged with main. A file-size regression in basic.rs was caught by local-validate.sh and fixed.

repo: File size limit is 1500 lines enforced by ./scripts/check-file-size.sh; crates/agents/src/runner/tests/basic.rs and crates/agents/src/model.rs are not in the allowlist.
Workspace lint config denies both clippy::unwrap_used and clippy::expect_used; tests must use unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(...)) pattern instead.

json_schema_ast canonicalization aggressively normalizes schemas: description-only or format-only properties are reduced to boolean `true`, so EnsurePropertyTypeTransform must run after canonicalization and handle the boolean-true case.
The openai_compat test suite lives in crates/providers/src/openai_compat/tests.rs, not inline or in a tests/ subdirectory.
local-validate.sh runs fmt, biome, tsc, zizmor, i18n parity, install-names, install-docs, and file-size checks in parallel; tsc requires npm install typescript first.
The transform pipeline order matters: EnsurePropertyTypeTransform must precede PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform so promoted properties survive pruning.
moltis-skills crate had a pre-existing clippy error in crates/skills/src/parse.rs that blocked clippy on dependent crates.

code.finding: EnsurePropertyTypeTransform must distinguish between bare boolean `true` schemas injected by canonicalization for orphaned required entries (should still be pruned) vs. object schemas with meaningful metadata missing only `type` (should be promoted to type:string). Only promote when the Value is an Object with keys.
Single-element vec![...] used with .iter() triggers clippy::useless_vec; use slice literal [...]  instead.
Inserting new test code at an edit boundary split a multi-line doc comment, leaving a dangling doc comment that triggers clippy::empty_line_after_doc_comments.

workflow: Run clippy on individual affected crates (cargo clippy -p crate-name) when a pre-existing error in a dependency blocks --all-targets.
When consolidating tests to meet line limits, merging positive+negative cases into one test function saves lines without losing coverage.
cargo fmt --all applies formatting in place; run it before committing rather than just checking.
local-validate.sh is the authoritative pre-push gate; running it catches file-size and tsc issues that clippy/tests miss.

friction: json_schema_ast canonicalization strips description/format-only schemas to boolean true before custom transforms run, requiring multiple test rewrites to find realistic test cases that survive the pipeline.
Workspace lint denies both unwrap and expect, but the pattern to use (unwrap_or_else(|| panic!())) is not documented; had to reverse-engineer from existing test code.
Clippy on affected crates was blocked by a pre-existing unrelated error in moltis-skills/src/parse.rs, requiring fixing it first.
cargo test filter syntax doesn't support pipe (|) for multiple test names; each test group requires a separate invocation.
Inserting code at edit boundaries in large test files split existing doc comments, causing an obscure clippy diagnostic.
basic.rs ended up at exactly 1501 lines after adding metadata: None fields — one line over the limit — requiring a second commit just to trim one blank line.
open_items: E2E tests for metadata round-tripping (tool-call-metadata.spec.js) and schema validation (schema-validation.spec.js) were intentionally deferred due to mock provider infrastructure complexity.
tsc check fails in local-validate.sh because typescript is not installed in the local npm environment; needs `npm install typescript` in the web UI directory.

The EnsurePropertyTypeTransform infers type:string as a fallback, which may not always be semantically correct — a more sophisticated inference based on property name or surrounding context could improve accuracy.
